mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 70% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market tightening (UNRATE < 5.5% and PAYEMS growth > 100k) raises wage pressures, increasing corporate costs and reducing equity earnings yields, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 5.5 and PAYEMS > 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.1) over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (RSAFS) amid stable inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2%) signals strong demand conditions, driving equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combination of RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL < 2% fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage inflation and prompting monetary policy tightening, which reduces equity valuations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 5.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 3% signals monetary tightening, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3%.
